context: So far every action has stood on its own - I work, I live. But real life is a sequence: you get up, then you eat, later you sleep. This is where you start describing your day in order.
explanation: Spanish splits the day into three parts: por la mañana (in the morning), por la tarde (in the afternoon) and por la noche (at night). Drop one onto a sentence you already know - "trabajo por la mañana" = I work in the morning - and suddenly you are saying when, not just what. Next you'll add the routine itself.

examples.note: por + part of the day = when something happens.
me levanto = I get up; a las + a number tells the time.
como here = I eat (same spelling as como = how).

examples.es: por la mañana desayuno y después trabajo
examples.en: in the morning I have breakfast and then I work
examples.note: después = then - it links two actions in order.
